---
title: "Untitled"
author: "Qingyang Du"
date: "2026-04-07"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(tidyverse)
library(survival)
library(tableone)
```


```{r}
################################################################################
# Framingham Heart Study - Interim Analysis Plan (Updated)
# Outcome: Incident Stroke | Separate models by Sex
# Follow-up: 10 years
# Forced: Age, Diabetes, SysBP
# Candidates: BPMEDS, CURSMOKE, TOTCHOL, BMI, PREVCHD
################################################################################

# ---- 1. Load and Prepare Data ------------------------------------------------

dat <- read.csv("frmgham2.csv")
dat$SEX <- factor(dat$SEX, levels = c(1, 2), labels = c("Male", "Female"))

baseline <- dat %>%
  filter(PERIOD == 1, PREVSTRK == 0)

data.frame(
  Total_Period1 = nrow(dat %>% filter(PERIOD == 1)),
  Excluded_PrevStroke = nrow(dat %>% filter(PERIOD == 1, PREVSTRK == 1)),
  Analytic_Cohort = nrow(baseline)
)

# ---- 2. Define 10-Year Outcome -----------------------------------------------

baseline <- baseline %>%
  mutate(
    time10 = pmin(TIMESTRK, 3652),
    stroke10 = ifelse(STROKE == 1 & TIMESTRK <= 3652, 1, 0)
  )

table(baseline$stroke10, baseline$SEX, dnn = c("Stroke", "Sex"))

# ---- 3. Missingness Summary --------------------------------------------------

all_vars <- c("AGE", "DIABETES", "SYSBP", "BPMEDS", "CURSMOKE",
              "TOTCHOL", "BMI", "PREVCHD", "TIMESTRK", "STROKE")

baseline %>%
  select(all_of(all_vars)) %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~sum(is.na(.)))) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "Variable", values_to = "N_Missing")

# ---- 4. Exploratory: Distributions ------------------------------------------

baseline %>%
  select(AGE, SYSBP, TOTCHOL, BMI) %>%
  summary()

sapply(c("AGE", "SYSBP", "TOTCHOL", "BMI"), function(v) {
  x <- na.omit(baseline[[v]])
  round(mean(((x - mean(x)) / sd(x))^3), 3)
})

# ---- 5. Correlation Among Covariates -----------------------------------------

baseline %>%
  select(AGE, SYSBP, BMI, TOTCHOL, DIABETES, BPMEDS, CURSMOKE, PREVCHD) %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), as.numeric)) %>%
  cor(use = "pairwise.complete.obs") %>%
  round(3)

# ---- 6. Table 1 -------------------------------------------------------------

table1_vars <- c("AGE", "SYSBP", "TOTCHOL", "BMI",
                 "DIABETES", "BPMEDS", "CURSMOKE", "PREVCHD")
cat_vars <- c("DIABETES", "BPMEDS", "CURSMOKE", "PREVCHD")

# Table 1a: By Sex
CreateTableOne(
  vars = table1_vars, strata = "SEX", data = baseline,
  factorVars = cat_vars, test = TRUE
) %>% print(showAllLevels = TRUE, formatOptions = list(big.mark = ","))

# Table 1b: By 10-Year Stroke Status
CreateTableOne(
  vars = table1_vars, strata = "stroke10", data = baseline,
  factorVars = cat_vars, test = TRUE
) %>% print(showAllLevels = TRUE, formatOptions = list(big.mark = ","))

# ---- 7. Events per sex (for modeling feasibility) ----------------------------

baseline %>%
  group_by(SEX) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    events = sum(stroke10),
    rate_pct = round(mean(stroke10) * 100, 2)
  )

# ---- 8. Risk Factor Change Across Exam Periods -------------------------------

dat_all3 <- dat %>%
  group_by(RANDID) %>%
  filter(n() == 3) %>%
  ungroup()

n_distinct(dat_all3$RANDID)

# SYSBP by Period
dat_all3 %>%
  group_by(PERIOD) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean = mean(SYSBP, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd = sd(SYSBP, na.rm = TRUE),
    median = median(SYSBP, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

# DIABETES Prevalence by Period
dat_all3 %>%
  group_by(PERIOD) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    n_diabetes = sum(DIABETES, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct = mean(DIABETES, na.rm = TRUE) * 100
  )

# SYSBP Change: Period 1 -> Period 3
wide_sysbp <- dat_all3 %>%
  select(RANDID, PERIOD, SYSBP) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = PERIOD, values_from = SYSBP, names_prefix = "P") %>%
  mutate(delta = P3 - P1)

summary(wide_sysbp$delta)

# Diabetes Transition: Period 1 -> Period 3
wide_diab <- dat_all3 %>%
  select(RANDID, PERIOD, DIABETES) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = PERIOD, values_from = DIABETES, names_prefix = "P")

table(wide_diab$P1, wide_diab$P3, dnn = c("Period1", "Period3"))


```




```{r}
################################################################################
# Framingham Heart Study - Interim Analysis Plan
# Outcome: Incident Stroke | Stratified by Sex
# Follow-up: 10 years | Covariates: Age, Diabetes, BMI, SysBP (baseline)
################################################################################

# ---- 1. Load and Prepare Data ------------------------------------------------

dat <- read.csv("frmgham2.csv")
dat$SEX <- factor(dat$SEX, levels = c(1, 2), labels = c("Male", "Female"))

baseline <- dat %>%
  filter(PERIOD == 1, PREVSTRK == 0)

data.frame(
  Total_Period1 = nrow(dat %>% filter(PERIOD == 1)),
  Excluded_PrevStroke = nrow(dat %>% filter(PERIOD == 1, PREVSTRK == 1)),
  Analytic_Cohort = nrow(baseline)
)

# ---- 2. Define 10-Year Outcome -----------------------------------------------

baseline <- baseline %>%
  mutate(
    time10 = pmin(TIMESTRK, 3652),
    stroke10 = ifelse(STROKE == 1 & TIMESTRK <= 3652, 1, 0)
  )

table(baseline$stroke10, baseline$SEX, dnn = c("Stroke", "Sex"))

# ---- 3. Missingness Summary --------------------------------------------------

baseline %>%
  select(AGE, DIABETES, BMI, SYSBP, TIMESTRK, STROKE) %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~sum(is.na(.)))) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "Variable", values_to = "N_Missing")

# ---- 4. Exploratory: Distributions & Outliers --------------------------------

baseline %>% select(AGE, SYSBP, BMI) %>% summary()

sapply(c("AGE", "SYSBP", "BMI"), function(v) {
  x <- na.omit(baseline[[v]])
  round(mean(((x - mean(x)) / sd(x))^3), 3)
})

# ---- 5. Correlation Among Covariates -----------------------------------------

baseline %>%
  select(AGE, SYSBP, BMI, DIABETES) %>%
  cor(use = "pairwise.complete.obs") %>%
  round(3)

# ---- 6. Table 1 (tableone) --------------------------------------------------
# NOTE: DIABETES, CURSMOKE, stroke10 are still numeric here — tableone needs that

table1_vars <- c("AGE", "SYSBP", "BMI", "DIABETES", "CURSMOKE")
cat_vars <- c("DIABETES", "CURSMOKE")

# Table 1a: By Sex
CreateTableOne(
  vars = table1_vars, strata = "SEX", data = baseline,
  factorVars = cat_vars, test = TRUE
) %>% print(showAllLevels = TRUE, formatOptions = list(big.mark = ","))

# Table 1b: By 10-Year Stroke Status
CreateTableOne(
  vars = table1_vars, strata = "stroke10", data = baseline,
  factorVars = cat_vars, test = TRUE
) %>% print(showAllLevels = TRUE, formatOptions = list(big.mark = ","))

# ---- 7. Risk Factor Change Across Exam Periods (Descriptive) -----------------

dat_all3 <- dat %>%
  group_by(RANDID) %>%
  filter(n() == 3) %>%
  ungroup()

n_distinct(dat_all3$RANDID)

# SYSBP by Period
dat_all3 %>%
  group_by(PERIOD) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean = mean(SYSBP, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd = sd(SYSBP, na.rm = TRUE),
    median = median(SYSBP, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

# DIABETES Prevalence by Period
dat_all3 %>%
  group_by(PERIOD) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    n_diabetes = sum(DIABETES, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct = mean(DIABETES, na.rm = TRUE) * 100
  )

# SYSBP Change: Period 1 -> Period 3
wide_sysbp <- dat_all3 %>%
  select(RANDID, PERIOD, SYSBP) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = PERIOD, values_from = SYSBP, names_prefix = "P") %>%
  mutate(delta = P3 - P1)

summary(wide_sysbp$delta)

# Diabetes Transition: Period 1 -> Period 3
wide_diab <- dat_all3 %>%
  select(RANDID, PERIOD, DIABETES) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = PERIOD, values_from = DIABETES, names_prefix = "P")

table(wide_diab$P1, wide_diab$P3, dnn = c("Period1", "Period3"))

# SYSBP Change: Period 1 -> Period 2
wide_sysbp <- dat_all3 %>%
  select(RANDID, PERIOD, SYSBP) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = PERIOD, values_from = SYSBP, names_prefix = "P") %>%
  mutate(delta = P2 - P1)

summary(wide_sysbp$delta)

# Diabetes Transition: Period 1 -> Period 2
wide_diab <- dat_all3 %>%
  select(RANDID, PERIOD, DIABETES) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = PERIOD, values_from = DIABETES, names_prefix = "P")

table(wide_diab$P1, wide_diab$P2, dnn = c("Period1", "Period2"))



```


```{r}



```
